text: This community service initiative addresses the need for economic independence and entrepreneurial character building among students at Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan. Utilizing an Asset-Based Community Development (ABCD) framework, the program leverages existing institutional resources—including food service units, bottled water production, and aquaculture—as practical laboratories for learning. The project engaged 10th and 11th-grade students through a structured methodology involving theoretical training in Sharia-compliant mu’amalah (economic transactions) and hands-on operational experience within the pesantren’s business units. Evaluation through pre-test and post-test assessments revealed a 37% improvement in student understanding, demonstrating that participants successfully learned to identify market opportunities and navigate business challenges within an Islamic ethical framework. By reframing the pesantren’s physical and human assets as drivers for growth, this initiative not only equipped students with vocational skills but also fostered a paradigm shift toward self-sufficiency. The results suggest that integrating religious education with practical entrepreneurship can effectively prepare students for post-pesantren life, providing a sustainable model for community-based economic empowerment in similar educational settings.
